danger_signals.visual_form: Cookies accumulating from top and right edges of playing field, grid expansion
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent game over condition; player failing to clear lines fast enough

danger_signals.visual_form: Progressive level advancement with more cookie types and denser arrangements
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating difficulty; higher cognitive demand and time pressure

safety_signals.visual_form: Matching cookies of same type disappearing from grid, visual feedback of removal
safety_signals.meaning: Player action achieving objective; progress toward level completion

safety_signals.visual_form: Yoshi-shaped cookie token appearing occasionally on grid
safety_signals.meaning: Player resource for flexible line completion; safety valve for difficult board states

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yoshi branding on title screen; Mario series IP integration

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: CPU-controlled cursor on split-screen in multiplayer VS
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Separate grid pane in split-screen layout; opponent side of interface

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Smaller grid size; fewer cookie types in rotation; slower spawn rate from edges
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI opponent (if present) plays with reduced efficiency; more time available per action

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Grid expands; additional cookie types introduced; denser arrangements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased spawn velocity; tighter time windows for line matching

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nearly full grid; maximum cookie type variety; complex pre-arranged patterns in Puzzle Mode
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapid grid accumulation; critical time pressure; one mistake leads to overflow

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fixed grid layout with specific cookie configuration; move counter displayed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must solve within maximum move limit; no spawn from edges; static board state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Split-screen layout; two independent grids side-by-side; opponent cursor visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player actions trigger disruption mechanics affecting opponent grid; simultaneous play

environmental_storytelling.example: Mario universe characters (Mario, Yoshi, Peach, Bowser) in cutscenes and sprite assets; Yoshi head as wild card token establishes diegetic connection
Cookie jar skit visual setup (NES/Game Boy versions, also attempted in SNES via unused assets) contextualizes cookie-clearing as thematic activity
BPS logo prominently displayed in intro sequence; different logo designs and musical stings between Japanese and international versions communicate publisher/developer identity
Grid size growth, cookie type proliferation, and move counter visibility (Puzzle Mode) signal escalating world complexity without narrative dialogue

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remain static on grid; accumulate from top/right edges in Action Mode
Visually disappear; trigger cascading board update
Functions as wild card; matches any cookie type in line completion
Rotates individual grid lines (rows/columns) like Rubik's Cube mechanic; visually highlights selected line
Cursor moves autonomously on opponent grid; disruption effects applied to player grid when opponent completes lines
Visual warning zone as cookies approach top edge; no explicit alert animation documented

notes: Yoshi's Cookie is a tile-matching puzzle game with minimal narrative world-state. Communication occurs primarily through grid mechanics, visual spawn rates, and difficulty progression. The game lacks traditional danger/safety signaling; instead, grid pressure (overflow threat) functions as implicit danger. Cutscenes feature Mario universe characters but appear minimal in final SNES release (unused assets suggest more elaborate opening was cut). The paradigm is pure mechanical puzzle with competitive VS dimension. No faction conflict, political situation, or morality system present. Visual communication is almost entirely UI/mechanic-driven rather than narrative-driven.
